items.title: Chelsea Wolfe Announces New Album The Dark: Hear “Cold”
items.summary: Last month, the goth-folk artist Chelsea Wolfe became the face of a new paranormal-themed perfume called Ghosts, which feels perfectly in line with Wolfe's entire aesthetic. At the same time, Wolfe shared two new songs, "Death Is Not The End" and "The Dark." As it turns out, both of those new songs are on a new Wolfe album, which is out next month and which is also called The Dark.
items.audio: audio/Chelsea Wolfe Announces New Album The Dark_ Hear .mp3

items.title: Boy Harsher Announce New Album Get Mean: Hear “Hard Beat”
items.summary: Darkwave fans will be feasting soon. Synthpop duo Boy Harsher, Jae Matthews and Augustus Muller, are returning with a new album, their first album since 2019's Careful and the original soundtrack they made for their horror film The Runner in 2022.
items.audio: audio/Boy Harsher Announce New Album Get Mean_ Hear _Har.mp3

items.title: Dog Date Return With New Surprise Album Perfectly
items.summary: It always sucks when you discover a cool band and then that band immediately breaks up. But on the other side, it's cool when that band then suddenly returns. Maybe they were gone for such a short period that you barely had time to miss them. Maybe they return with a brand-new surprise album. With all that in mind, Dog Date are back. The post Dog Date Return With New Surprise Album <em>Perfectly</em> appeared first on Stereogum.
items.audio: audio/Dog Date Return With New Surprise Album Perfectly.mp3

items.title: Missio Open Up About Their Latest Album, Love & Heartbreak
items.summary: Missio, an alternative electronic duo out of Austin, Texas, composed of Matthew Brue on vocals and David Butler behind the boards, released their sixth record, Love & Heartbreak, on July 3rd through Nettwerk. Sonically, it’s a conscious return to minimal electronic production, guitars are largely retired and dark synths carry the tracks. Lyrically, it’s their […]

items.title: Reading Isn’t ‘Over.’ Because Reading Was Never the Issue
items.summary: When I was in kindergarten, we were allowed a short, midday recess where we could run around and play tag or kick a ball and have fun with our friends. I always opted to borrow a book from the over-painted, wooden cubby-style class library, and sit cross-legged under a shady tree to quietly read.  One […]

items.title: Chelsea Wolfe talks new album ‘The Dark’: “I needed to go through some difficult times to stop self-abandoning”
items.summary: The Californian cult favourite tells NME why her newly-announced ninth album pays homage to the “gothic heart” of her artistry, the “deeper work” that followed her sobriety, reuniting with a Queens Of The Stone Age member and learning to embrace the second half of her life The post Chelsea Wolfe talks new album ‘The Dark’: “I needed to go through some difficult times to stop self-abandoning” appeared first on NME.
